title: श्री वामन स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री वामन स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ नमो भगवते वामनाय।
त्रिविक्रमाय ब्रह्मचारिणे विष्णवे नमः॥

वामनं प्रणमाम्यादिं त्रिविक्रममनुत्तमम्।
त्रिभिः क्रमैर्जगत्सर्वमाक्रम्य परमेश्वरम्॥ १॥

अदित्यां कश्यपाज्जातं दिव्यब्रह्मचारिणम्।
वटुरूपधरं विष्णुं छत्रदण्डकमण्डलुम्॥ २॥

बलियज्ञसभामध्ये विप्ररूपेण संस्थितम्।
त्रिपादभूमिं याचन्तं बलेर्दानवपुङ्गवात्॥ ३॥

प्रथमेन पदेनैव सम्पूर्णां पृथिवीमिमाम्।
द्वितीयेन दिवं सर्वं त्रिलोकीं व्याप्य तिष्ठति॥ ४॥

तृतीयपादविन्यासं बलिमस्तकमागतम्।
पातालं प्रेषयामास दत्त्वा चिरजीवनम्॥ ५॥

त्रिविक्रमः स भगवान् विश्वरूपं प्रदर्शयन्।
ब्रह्मादिभिः स्तुतः सर्वैर्जयजयेति निनादयन्॥ ६॥

पञ्चमं दशावताराणां वामनाख्यमनुत्तमम्।
उपेन्द्रमिन्द्रसहजं वामनं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

श्रीवत्साङ्कं घनश्यामं पीताम्बरधरं हरिम्।
वामनं वटुवेषं तं लोकपालं नमाम्यहम्॥ ८॥

बलिप्रदं दानवीरं शुक्रशिष्यनिवारकम्।
दैत्येन्द्रदर्पहन्तारं वामनं शरणं व्रजे॥ ९॥

इन्द्राय स्वर्गमादाय प्रयच्छन्तं जगत्पतिम्।
दानधर्मप्रवक्तारं वामनं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ १०॥

वामनस्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेद् भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥ ११॥

॥ इति श्री वामन स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vamana Stotram ||

Om Namo Bhagavate Vamanaya |
Trivikramaya Brahmacharine Vishnave Namah ||

Vamanam pranamami-adim Trivikramam-anuttamam |
Tribhih kramaih jagat-sarvam-akramya Parameshwaram || 1 ||

Adityam Kashyapaj-jatam divya-brahmacharinam |
Vatu-rupa-dharam Vishnum Chhatra-danda-kamandalum || 2 ||

Bali-yajna-sabha-madhye vipra-rupena samsthitam |
Tripada-bhumim yachantam Baler-danava-pungavat || 3 ||

Prathamena padenai-va sampurnam prithivim-imam |
Dvitiyena divam sarvam triloki vyapya tishthati || 4 ||

Tritiya-pada-vinyasam Bali-mastakam-agatam |
Patalam preshayamasa dattva chira-jivanam || 5 ||

Trivikramah sa Bhagavan Vishva-rupam pradarshayan |
Brahmadibhih stutah sarvaih jaya-jayeti ninadayan || 6 ||

Panchamam Dasha-avataranam Vamana-akhyam-anuttamam |
Upendram-Indra-sahajam Vamanam pranamami-aham || 7 ||

Shrivatsa-ankam Ghana-shyamam Pitambara-dharam Harim |
Vamanam Vatu-vesham tam Loka-palam namamyaham || 8 ||

Bali-pradam Dana-viram Shukra-shishya-nivarakam |
Daityendra-darpa-hantaram Vamanam sharanam vraje || 9 ||

Indraya svargam-adaya prayachchhantam Jagatpatim |
Dana-dharma-pravaktaram Vamanam pranamami-aham || 10 ||

Vamana-stotram-idam punyam yah pathed bhakti-samyutah |
Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Vishnulokam sa gachchhati || 11 ||

|| Iti Shri Vamana Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: तीन पगों से सम्पूर्ण जगत को आक्रम्य करने वाले त्रिविक्रम वामन को प्रणाम। अदिति-कश्यप से जन्मे, दिव्य ब्रह्मचारी, छत्र-दण्ड-कमण्डलु धारी वटुरूपी विष्णु। बलि यज्ञ सभा में विप्ररूप से त्रिपाद भूमि याचना की। प्रथम पद से पृथ्वी, द्वितीय से स्वर्ग व्याप्त किया। तृतीय पाद बलि के मस्तक पर रखकर पाताल भेजा किन्तु चिरजीवन दिया। पंचम दशावतार, उपेन्द्र, इन्द्रसहज वामन को नमन। दैत्येन्द्र दर्प हन्ता, दानधर्म प्रवक्ता वामन की शरण। इस स्तोत्र के पाठ से सर्वपापमुक्ति और विष्णुलोक प्राप्ति होती है।
benefits: Invokes the expansive power of Lord Trivikrama
Traditionally recited for removal of pride, ego, and arrogance
Invokes the merit of charitable giving (dana)
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants liberation
Invokes the blessing of vishnuloka after death
Traditionally invoked for protection from enemies and grants victory
description: The Vamana Stotram, known in Sanskrit as श्री वामन स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Vishnu. This sacred composition is sourced from Vamana Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to protect from enemies and grant victory, destroy pride, ego, and arrogance, and destroy all sins and grant liberation. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Vamana Jayanti and Onam. The text contains 11 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Vamana Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vamana Stotram in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to Lord Vishnu's Vamana (dwarf) and Trivikrama avatar.

Vamana Stotram Kashyapa Muni Brihat Naradiya Purana

seoFaqs.question: Where is the Vamana Stotram traditionally recorded?
seoFaqs.answer: The most cited Vamana Stotram is preserved in the Brihat Naradiya Purana and is spoken by Sage Kashyapa, the celestial father of Vamana himself. Beyond this canonical version, the eighth canto of the Bhagavata Purana (chapters 15-23) narrates the full Vamana avatar story along with several embedded stuti passages by Aditi, Brahma and the assembled devas as Vamana grows into the cosmic Trivikrama. Each variant focuses on different aspects: the Kashyapa stotram extols Vamana's playful dwarf form and humility, while the Bhagavata stutis emphasise the Trivikrama theophany. Both forms are recited on Vamana Jayanti and Vamana Dwadashi. (Sources: Drikpanchang Lord Vamana; Wikipedia Vamana; AstroVed.)

seoFaqs.question: Why did Vishnu descend as a dwarf rather than in a powerful form?
seoFaqs.answer: King Bali, grandson of Prahlada, had earned through penance and Ashvamedha sacrifices a level of dharmic merit that made him invincible to direct combat with the devas. The traditional commentaries explain that meeting such a virtuous king with force would have been adharmic; instead, Vishnu chose the brahmin dwarf form to test Bali's celebrated generosity. By asking only for three paces of land and then expanding into the cosmic Trivikrama who stepped over earth, heaven and finally Bali's own head to send him to Sutala-loka, Vishnu honoured Bali's word, neutralised the threat to deva sovereignty, and granted Bali a higher boon than he could have achieved through victory. (Sources: Gayatri Heritage; Drikpanchang; AstroVed.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Vamana Stotram and the festival of Onam?
seoFaqs.answer: In Kerala, Onam is celebrated as the annual visit of King Mahabali to his former subjects after his exile to Sutala by Vamana. The Vamana avatar story, which culminates in Vishnu placing his foot on Bali's head, is therefore the foundational narrative of Onam and is retold in temple discourses across the ten-day festival. While Tamil and Telugu traditions emphasise Vamana Dwadashi as the avatar's appearance day in Bhadrapada Shukla, Kerala's Trikkakara Vamanamoorthy Temple in Ernakulam district is regarded as the central Vamana shrine where Onam celebrations originate, and the Vamana Stotram is part of the festival liturgy. (Sources: Wikipedia Vamana; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the Trivikrama symbolism in the stotram represent philosophically?
seoFaqs.answer: The three steps of Trivikrama are interpreted on multiple levels. Cosmologically, they cover the three lokas of bhuh, bhuvah and svah, asserting Vishnu's pervasion of all space. Vedantically, drawing on the Rig Vedic hymn idam vishnur vichakrame tredha (RV 1.22.17), the three steps represent the three states of waking, dream and deep sleep through which the witness consciousness moves, or the past, present and future of time itself. Bhakti commentators read the third step on Bali's head as the moment of complete surrender, where the ego is touched by the Lord's foot and liberated. The Vamana Stotram therefore moves from praising the small charming dwarf to invoking the all-pervading cosmic principle. (Sources: Gayatri Heritage; AstroVed; Wikipedia.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Vamana Stotram traditionally recited and what benefits are claimed?
seoFaqs.answer: The most auspicious recitation occasions are Vamana Jayanti (Bhadrapada Shukla Dwadashi, Vamana's appearance day), Vamana Dwadashi observed monthly, Onam in Kerala, Sravana Dwadashi, and any Ekadashi-Dvadashi sequence dedicated to Vishnu. The phalashruti attached to the Brihat Naradiya version promises removal of obstacles raised by powerful adversaries, growth of intelligence and humility in young students, blessing of dwarfism or developmental issues in children, and fulfilment of land or property aspirations. Devotees observing Vamana Dwadashi traditionally fast on Ekadashi, recite the stotram at sunrise, and offer curd-rice and yellow flowers to Vamana before breaking the fast. (Sources: AstroVed; Drikpanchang; Gayatri Heritage.)

seoFaqs.question: Is Vamana Stotram appropriate for daily recitation or only festivals?
seoFaqs.answer: Both. The compact length of the Kashyapa version (typically 8 to 12 verses) makes it suitable for daily recitation as part of a Dashavatara cycle, where one stotram from each of the ten avatars is chanted in sequence. Many Vaishnava households in Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka and Kerala include Vamana Stotram in their morning Vishnu-puja alongside Vishnu Sahasranama. Daily recitation is said to cultivate the virtues Vamana embodies, namely humility, respectful speech, and the wisdom to ask for what is sufficient rather than what is excessive, while festival-day recitation is performed for the larger phala of land, progeny and victory over arrogant adversaries. (Sources: Gayatri Heritage; AstroVed.)
